objective: Design and implement a reliable, scalable pipeline to load the employees table from the MySQL agentichub database (Cloud SQL: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore) into BigQuery (verizon-data.verizon_data_dea.employees), with a one-time full historical load followed by ongoing daily scheduled incremental loads, supporting analytics, reporting, and long-term storage for the 5gcore programme.
business_context: The 5gcore programme requires a reliable MySQL-to-BigQuery data pipeline to make employee data available for analytics, reporting, and long-term storage. Stakeholder Yash requires full historical data completeness as a validated baseline before incremental loads are enabled. The dataset is estimated at 5 GB with 1 GB/month growth and a 60-day retention policy. Key business outcomes include stakeholder confidence in data quality and independent stakeholder access to load validation reports without engineering assistance.
functional_requirements: Build a batch data ingestion pipeline from MySQL agentichub.employees to BigQuery verizon_data_dea.employees
Perform a one-time full historical load of all records from MySQL agentichub.employees into BigQuery verizon_data_dea.employees
Implement chunking or batching strategy to handle 5 GB data volume and prevent timeouts and memory failures during historical load
Implement retry logic for failed extraction chunks during the historical load
Set up daily scheduled incremental loads from MySQL to BigQuery following historical load sign-off
Block incremental pipeline activation until historical load validation sign-off is formally documented
Implement pre-load schema validation covering field names, data types, and null handling
Perform post-load row count reconciliation between MySQL and BigQuery after every load execution
Enforce primary key uniqueness checks in BigQuery employees table after every load
Validate null and datatype constraints against schema definitions before each load
Define and enforce a handling strategy for new or unexpected MySQL columns prior to their inclusion in loads
Maintain audit logs for every historical and incremental load execution
Configure monitoring and alerting for pipeline failures with early detection capability
Provide stakeholders with visibility into load success and data quality via logs or a self-service dashboard

source_systems: MySQL Cloud SQL (agentichub database, employees table)
data_requirements.volume: 5 GB initial load; 1 GB/month growth; 60-day data retention policy
data_requirements.frequency: One-time full historical load on-demand; daily scheduled incremental loads thereafter

non_functional.performance: Historical load must process 5 GB using chunking/batching to avoid timeouts and memory failures, and must complete within sprint 14 timeline
non_functional.scalability: Pipeline must accommodate 1 GB/month ongoing data growth
non_functional.sla: Monitoring alerts must fire within acceptable SLA on any pipeline failure

security.access_controls: Source MySQL connection authenticated with username 'sa'; target is BigQuery project verizon-data under dataset verizon_data_dea

constraints.timeline: Historical load must complete within sprint 14 timeline
constraints.technical_limitations: 5 GB initial data volume requires chunking or batching strategy to prevent timeouts and memory failures during extraction

assumptions: Incremental load strategy will use a watermark or timestamp-based approach on the employees table
BigQuery target table schema will mirror the MySQL source schema with appropriate type mappings
Sign-off for historical load validation will be provided by stakeholder Yash before incremental loads are activated
Audit logs will be stored in a location accessible to stakeholders such as a BigQuery audit table or reporting dashboard
The 60-day data retention policy applies to the BigQuery target dataset verizon_data_dea
inferred_assumptions: non_functional.latency: Not specified in requirements; inferred as not a binding constraint given the daily batch ingestion schedule
acceptance_criteria: All agreed-upon MySQL tables are present and queryable in BigQuery
Historical data is 100% complete with row counts verified between MySQL source and BigQuery target
Scheduled incremental loads execute on time without failures
Incremental pipeline does not activate until historical load is validated and sign-off is formally documented
Row counts must match between MySQL source and BigQuery target with zero tolerance for missing records
No records are skipped or duplicated during chunked historical load execution
Primary key uniqueness is enforced in BigQuery employees table after every load
Null and datatype constraints are validated against schema definitions before each load
Historical load completes within sprint 14 timeline
No primary key duplicates detected in BigQuery target after any load execution
All schema mismatches are logged and resolved per a defined strategy
Monitoring alerts fire within acceptable SLA on any pipeline failure
Stakeholders can access and understand load validation reports without engineering assistance
New MySQL columns are logged and require defined strategy approval before being included in any load
expected_outputs: Fully loaded BigQuery table verizon-data.verizon_data_dea.employees with complete historical records from MySQL agentichub.employees
Daily scheduled incremental load pipeline transferring new and updated records from MySQL to BigQuery
Row count reconciliation reports validating data completeness after every load execution
Audit log covering all historical and incremental load executions
Monitoring and alerting configuration for pipeline failure detection within SLA
Stakeholder-accessible load validation dashboard or report for independent quality review
Documented historical load validation sign-off prior to incremental pipeline activation
raw_text:
Ticket: SCRUM-145
Summary: build mysql to bigquery data ingestion pipeline
Status: To Do
Priority: Medium
Assignee: Shailendra Singh
Description:
project name: 5gcore requirement type: new_dvlp stakeholder: yash schedule interval: daily objective design and implement a reliable and scalable pipeline to load mysql tables into bigquery, supporting analytics, reporting, and long-term storage for the business design and implement a reliable, scalable pipeline to load the employees table from the mysql agentichub database (cloud sql: verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore) into bigquery (verizon-data.verizon_data_dea.employees). requires a one-time full historical load followed by ongoing scheduled incremental loads. estimated data size is 5 gb with 1 gb/month growth and a 60-day data retention policy. pipeline will support analytics, reporting, and long-term storage for the 5gcore programme. expected outcome all agreed-upon tables fully loaded into bigquery with complete historical data, reliable scheduled data refresh, and stakeholder confidence in data quality connections and db details source: mysql cloud sql connection verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore, ip: 34.70.79.163, port: 3306, db: agentichub, table: employees, user: sa; target: bigquery project verizon-data, dataset: verizon_data_dea, table: employees; data size: 5 gb, growth: 1 gb/month, retention: 60 days acceptance criteria success conditions: all agreed-upon mysql tables are present and queryable in bigquery historical data is 100% complete with row counts verified scheduled loads execute on time without failures incremental pipeline does not activate until historical load is validated and signed off validation rules: row counts must match between mysql source and bigquery target primary key uniqueness must be enforced in bigquery null and datatype constraints must be validated against schema definitions incremental loads must not begin until historical load validation sign-off is documented new mysql columns must follow a defined handling strategy before load proceeds

--- Related Tickets (2) ---

Related Ticket: SCRUM-146
Summary: implement one-time full historical load from mysql to bigquery
Status: To Do
Priority: Medium
Description:
project name: 5gcore requirement type: new_dvlp stakeholder: yash schedule interval: on-demand objective ensure 100% historical data completeness in bigquery before enabling incremental loads, which is critical for accurate business reporting perform a one-time complete extraction of all records from the mysql agentichub.employees table and load into bigquery verizon_data_dea.employees. must handle the estimated 5 gb data volume using a chunking or batching strategy to avoid timeouts and memory failures, with retry logic for failed extractions. historical load must be fully validated by record count reconciliation and spot checks before incremental pipeline activation is permitted. expected outcome all historical records from mysql agentichub.employees fully loaded into bigquery with validated row counts and zero data loss, providing the baseline for incremental loads connections and db details source: mysql (verizon-data:us-central1:mysql-druid-metadatastore), ip: 34.70.79.163, port: 3306, db: agentichub, table: employees, user: sa; target: bigquery verizon-data.verizon_data_dea.employees; estimated data size: 5 gb acceptance criteria success conditions: row counts match between mysql and bigquery post-load no data loss or duplication during chunked extraction and load historical load completes within sprint 14 timeline sign-off documented before incremental pipeline is activated validation rules: row counts must match between source and target with zero tolerance for missing records no records may be skipped or duplicated during chunked load execution incremental pipeline must not activate until historical validation sign-off is formally recorded

Related Ticket: SCRUM-147
Summary: set up data quality validation, schema checks, monitoring, and audit logging
Status: To Do
Priority: Medium
Description:
project name: 5gcore requirement type: new_dvlp stakeholder: yash schedule interval: daily objective ensure data integrity and stakeholder confidence in the pipeline through comprehensive validation checks, full audit trails, and proactive monitoring implement a comprehensive data quality and observability framework for the mysql to bigquery pipeline. covers pre-load schema validation (field names, data types, null handling), post-load row count reconciliation, primary key uniqueness checks, and datatype constraint verification. maintain audit logs for every load execution (historical and incremental). configure monitoring and alerting for pipeline failures for early detection, and provide stakeholders with visibility into load success via logs or a dashboard. expected outcome all load executions validated with full audit trails, monitoring alerts detecting failures early, and stakeholders able to independently review data quality and load outcomes connections and db details source: mysql agentichub.employees; target: bigquery verizon_data_dea.employees; audit logs covering all historical and incremental load runs acceptance criteria success conditions: row counts match between mysql and bigquery after every load execution no primary key duplicates detected in bigquery target all schema mismatches logged and resolved per defined strategy monitoring alerts fire within acceptable sla on any pipeline failure stakeholders can access and understand load validation reports without engineering assistance validation rules: row counts must match between source mysql and target bigquery with zero tolerance primary keys must be unique in the bigquery employees table after every load null and datatype constraints must be validated against schema definitions before each load new mysql columns must be logged and require a defined strategy approval before being included in loads
